steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The blank describes Nancy Bird-Walton's place in historical memory compared to Chojnowska-Liskiewicz's. The key contrast: Chojnowska-Liskiewicz "will always be remembered" — her legacy is permanent and secure. The blank must describe something weaker or less stable than that.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'deserving': Speaks to moral worthiness, not the security of historical memory. Doesn't contrast with "will always be remembered." ✗
  • Choice B — 'conspicuous': Means highly visible or prominent — this would put Nancy's legacy on par with or above Chojnowska-Liskiewicz's, not below it. Wrong direction. ✗
  • Choice C — 'uncertain': Means not firmly established. Directly contrasts with "will always be remembered" — Nancy's historical memory is less secure, which matches the passage's hedging ("perhaps more ______"). ✓
  • Choice D — 'enduring': Means lasting — this would imply Nancy's legacy outlasts Chojnowska-Liskiewicz's, the opposite of what the passage implies. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
